message:
fix: remove signal-exit SIGINT listeners to prevent Ctrl+C death

Ink uses signal-exit which registers a SIGINT handler that re-raises
the signal to kill the process. In Bun, setRawMode may not fully
suppress OS-level SIGINT generation from Ctrl+C (unlike Node which
clears the ISIG termios flag). Remove all SIGINT listeners after ink
initializes and register a no-op, so Ctrl+C is handled exclusively
by InputArea's useInput two-stage logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
